name: Cornbread-Sausage Stuffing with Apples
recipeIngredient: 3 Apples, cored (Jonathan and Winesap are good choices)
recipeInstructions:
1. Core apples and cut into chunks (do not peel). Choose Jonathan or Winesap apples for best flavor.
2. Melt half the butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add chopped yellow onions and cook until tender and lightly colored, about 25 minutes. Transfer onions and melted butter to a large mixing bowl.
3. Melt remaining butter in the same skillet over high heat. Add apple chunks and cook until lightly colored but not mushy. Transfer apples and melted butter to the mixing bowl.
4. Crumble sausage into the skillet and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned. Remove sausage with a slotted spoon and reserve the rendered fat in the skillet.
5. Add remaining ingredients (cornbread, bread types, thyme, sage, salt and pepper, parsley, and pecans) to the mixing bowl and gently combine.
6. Allow stuffing to cool completely before using. If not used promptly, refrigerate it.
7. To stuff a bird (such as turkey), spoon the mixture into the cavity. For an alternative, place the stuffing in a casserole dish, cover, and set in a large pan with about 1/2 inch of water covering halfway up the sides. Bake at 325°F for 30 to 45 minutes, basting occasionally with cooking juices or reserved sausage fat if needed.
Enough stuffing for a 20-pound turkey yields 12 to 14 portions.
